# omada_port_forward (Resource)

Manages a NAT port-forwarding rule on the Omada gateway (Settings → Transmission → NAT → Port Forwarding).

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Forward WAN :443 to the homelab ingress on the LAN.
resource "omada_port_forward" "https" {
  name          = "Homelab"
  external_port = "443"
  forward_ip    = "10.10.20.95"
  forward_port  = "443"
  protocol      = "tcp"
  # wan_port_ids defaults to the WAN port used by existing rules.
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `external_port` (String) External (WAN) port or range, e.g. `443` or `8000-8010`.
- `forward_ip` (String) Internal LAN IP to forward to.
- `forward_port` (String) Internal port or range.
- `name` (String) Rule name.
- `protocol` (String) One of `tcp`, `udp`, or `tcp_udp`.

### Optional

- `dmz` (Boolean) Whether this is a DMZ rule.
- `enable` (Boolean) Whether the rule is enabled.
- `site` (String) Site name. Defaults to the primary site. Changing this forces replacement.
- `wan_port_ids` (List of String) WAN port(s) the rule applies to. Defaults to the WAN port used by existing rules; populated on import.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Controller-assigned rule ID.
- `site_id` (String) Resolved site ID.
